title: Entity-store structural command ordering repair
summary: Replace the inversion-dependent structural command ordering bottleneck with deterministic preallocated bounded ordering so the unchanged canonical entity-store gate passes without touching registry, component, benchmark or baseline semantics.
acceptance: Replace StructuralCommandBuffer.orderQueuedSlots inversion-dependent insertion sorting with deterministic bounded ordering whose work is linear in queued commands times a fixed key-pass count; Array.sort, unbounded comparison sorting and retry-based performance behavior are prohibited

The replacement orders every command exactly by command priority, signed Int32 entity index and insertion sequence, preserving the existing destroy, detach-i32, attach-i32, set-i32 and allocate precedence plus stable same-kind/same-index insertion order

All ordering scratch and bucket storage is allocated once when the StructuralCommandBuffer is constructed and reused across commits; the ordering stage creates no per-command or per-commit object, array, typed-array, closure or constructed-string allocation

Existing queue, commit and result APIs, structured reasons, result ordering, stale-handle behavior, capacity failures, entity generation/reuse semantics and Int32 component semantics remain byte/behavior compatible; no public export, save, protocol or snapshot contract changes

Focused tests cover mixed priorities, reverse indexes, repeated same-index commands, signed/invalid stored indexes, capacity boundaries and consecutive buffer reuse while proving the exact historical priority/index/sequence result order
The unchanged entity-store workload retains capacity 8192, queuedCommands 8192, commitResultCount 8192, appliedCommands 8192, failedCommands 0, attachedComponents 8192 and iterationChecksum 134209536

After one approved quiet-host admission, corepack pnpm bench --filter entity-store runs exactly once with the unchanged 244.507 ms baseline, 10/20 percent thresholds, 9 samples and 2 warmups; every invariant matches and the median is at most 293.4084 ms so the gate does not fail
M1 hauling/building, M2 work/logistics and M3 ordinary-life 100000-tick runs retain hashes 0xf7815189, 0x6943dad4 and 0x7eb81a69 and report no invariant drift

packages/sim-core/src/entity-id.ts and packages/sim-core/src/component-store.ts remain unchanged. If the structural ordering repair does not make the single authorized entity-store run pass, stop and taskctl block with new exact evidence instead of widening this task
No benchmark workload, baseline, threshold, sample/warmup count, benchmark harness, sim-protocol, Worker, GameSession, application, dependency, release or WM-0177/WM-0170 surface changes

history.details: Authorized from the quiet-host WM-0178 blocker at evidence commit 0b89918. Scope is limited to deterministic preallocated structural command ordering and one focused invariant test; entity registry, component store, benchmark workload/baseline and every downstream task remain unchanged.

forbiddenPaths: packages/sim-core/src/entity-id.ts and packages/sim-core/src/component-store.ts unless WM-0179 first blocks with new exact evidence and systems architecture creates a separately reviewed scope
packages/sim-core/src/structural-command-types.ts, packages/sim-core/src/index.ts or any public API/export change
packages/benchmarks/** including entity-store workload, packages/benchmarks/baseline.json, invariant payloads, 10/20 thresholds and default 9-sample/2-warmup counts
Array.sort, unbounded comparison sorting, inversion-dependent insertion shifting, per-commit scratch allocation, failure suppression, warm-up retry or retry-until-pass
packages/sim-protocol/**, packages/sim-worker/**, apps/**, renderer, UI, Electron, GameSession, save/load, content, dependencies, release or deployment files
coordination/artifacts/WM-0083/** or any historical benchmark artifact
unblocking, claiming or editing WM-0178, WM-0177 or WM-0170 from the WM-0179 worker lane
executionLane: simulation-engineer only; Spark is prohibited because deterministic structural ordering, hot-path allocation behavior and canonical performance attribution require full implementation evidence and independent review
performanceRunPolicy: After implementation and non-benchmark checks, use only Get-Process CPU deltas for two consecutive six-second windows. Both windows must be at most 40 percent normalized host busy, every single-process host share at most 8 percent, ACShadows absent, no active benchmark Node and git clean. Then run the entity-store filter exactly once. Do not use Get-Counter, CIM or WMI for admission; do not pre-warm, retry or run a second performance attempt.
fullGateOwner: WM-0178 retains the isolated canonical full-suite gate. WM-0179 proves only the exact product-bound entity-store filter; after integration WM-0178 must verify every filtered and full canonical gate on the repaired main lineage.
resumeClosure: WM-0179 must receive independent reviewer verification, project-director integration and done on main.

Under project-director control, refresh the existing WM-0178 branch from repaired main while preserving only its reviewed harness/evidence diff and auditing conflicts; do not create a linked worktree.

Systems architecture may then unblock WM-0178 for the original qa-performance owner. WM-0178 must run its six explicit filters once each and the exact full corepack pnpm bench once, with no retries or waiver, then pass all remaining checks and independent review.

Only after WM-0178 is integrated and done may the original simulation-engineer resume WM-0177 under its existing paired-attribution procedure; WM-0170 remains blocked until WM-0177 is independently verified, integrated and done.
singleWriterRule: WM-0179 is the sole product writer while active. WM-0178 remains blocked and claimed by qa-performance with no harness/evidence writes; WM-0177 remains blocked and claimed by its original simulation-engineer with no product writes; WM-0170 remains blocked.
